title: Add optional uWSGI field to Keystone CRD
description:
**Size:** 📦 medium
**Category:** backend
**Priority:** medium
**Source:** Proposed for 'Make uwsgi flags configurable via the CRD or a con'

Add a new UWSGISpec struct (Processes int32 min=1 default=2, Threads int32 min=1 default=2, HTTPKeepAlive bool default=true) to api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go as an optional *UWSGISpec pointer field on KeystoneSpec, following the existing *FederationSpec/*PolicySpec pattern. Wire webhook defaulting in KeystoneWebhook.Default() for zero-valued sub-fields when spec.uwsgi is non-nil. Modify buildKeystoneDeployment in reconcile_deployment.go to read keystone.Spec.UWSGI and construct the uWSGI command dynamically, falling back to current hardcoded defaults (processes=2, threads=2, keepalive=true) when the field is nil. Regenerate CRD manifests via make generate && make manifests. Add table-driven unit tests in reconcile_deployment_test.go covering nil uwsgi (defaults preserved), custom values, and keepalive on/off. Add integration test verifying Deployment container args reflect CRD uwsgi values.

**Rationale:** The uWSGI worker processes, threads, and HTTP keep-alive flags are currently hardcoded at reconcile_deployment.go:157-168. Operators running Keystone under varying load profiles cannot tune connection concurrency without modifying operator source code and rebuilding. Exposing these as CRD fields gives platform teams a declarative, auditable knob for performance tuning while preserving safe defaults for standard deployments.

**Affected Areas:**
- operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go
- operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_webhook.go
- operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go
- operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment_test.go
- operators/keystone/config/crd/bases/

stories.title: Operator tunes uWSGI worker processes and threads via CRD
stories.want: to set spec.uwsgi.processes and spec.uwsgi.threads on the Keystone CR
stories.so_that: I can tune uWSGI concurrency to match my workload profile without rebuilding the operator
criteria: Setting spec.uwsgi.processes=4 and spec.uwsgi.threads=4 produces --processes 4 --threads 4 in the Deployment container command
The Deployment rolls out new pods with the updated uWSGI command args
The Keystone CR is accepted by the validating webhook with valid uwsgi values
Changes to spec.uwsgi trigger a rolling update of existing pods

stories.title: Operator disables HTTP keep-alive via CRD
stories.want: to set spec.uwsgi.httpKeepAlive=false on the Keystone CR
stories.so_that: I can disable HTTP keep-alive for environments where it causes connection pooling issues
criteria: Setting spec.uwsgi.httpKeepAlive=false omits --http-keepalive from the Deployment container command
Setting spec.uwsgi.httpKeepAlive=true (or leaving default) includes --http-keepalive in the command
The Deployment rolls out with the updated command when httpKeepAlive changes

stories.title: Keystone CR without spec.uwsgi preserves current defaults
stories.want: Keystone CRs that omit spec.uwsgi to continue working with the current hardcoded defaults
stories.so_that: existing deployments are not disrupted by the new field
criteria: A Keystone CR with no spec.uwsgi field produces --processes 2 --threads 2 --http-keepalive in the Deployment command
Upgrading the operator with existing CRs that lack spec.uwsgi does not change the Deployment command
The webhook does NOT inject a default UWSGISpec when spec.uwsgi is nil (preserving backward compatibility)

stories.title: Webhook rejects invalid uWSGI configuration
stories.want: to receive clear validation errors when I submit invalid uWSGI values
stories.so_that: I can correct my configuration before it reaches the reconciler
criteria: Setting spec.uwsgi.processes=0 is rejected by CRD schema validation (kubebuilder Minimum=1)
Setting spec.uwsgi.threads=0 is rejected by CRD schema validation (kubebuilder Minimum=1)
Defense-in-depth webhook validation rejects processes < 1 and threads < 1 with field-specific error messages
Negative values for processes or threads are rejected

stories.title: Webhook defaults zero-valued sub-fields when spec.uwsgi is set
stories.want: to set only the uwsgi fields I care about and have the rest defaulted
stories.so_that: I don't need to specify every field when I only want to change one
criteria: Setting spec.uwsgi: {} results in processes=2, threads=2, httpKeepAlive=true after webhook defaulting
Setting spec.uwsgi.processes=4 with threads unset results in threads=2 after defaulting
Setting spec.uwsgi.httpKeepAlive=false with processes/threads unset results in processes=2, threads=2 after defaulting
Explicitly set values are never overwritten by the defaulting webhook

requirements.description: The CRD SHALL define a UWSGISpec struct with Processes (int32, min=1, default=2), Threads (int32, min=1, default=2), and HTTPKeepAlive (bool, default=true) fields, exposed as an optional *UWSGISpec pointer field spec.uwsgi on KeystoneSpec
requirements.rationale: Follows the established optional pointer field pattern (FederationSpec, PolicySpec, AutoscalingSpec, Resources) for fields that have safe defaults when absent

scenarios.name: UWSGISpec struct registered in CRD schema
scenarios.when: make manifests is run after adding the UWSGISpec type
scenarios.then: the generated CRD YAML contains spec.uwsgi with processes (integer, minimum=1, default=2), threads (integer, minimum=1, default=2), and httpKeepAlive (boolean, default=true) properties

scenarios.name: spec.uwsgi is optional
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR is submitted without spec.uwsgi
scenarios.then: the CR is accepted without validation errors

scenarios.name: CRD schema rejects processes below minimum
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR is submitted with spec.uwsgi.processes=0
scenarios.then: the API server rejects the CR with a schema validation error before it reaches the webhook

requirements.description: The defaulting webhook SHALL populate zero-valued sub-fields of spec.uwsgi when the pointer is non-nil: Processes=2 if 0, Threads=2 if 0, HTTPKeepAlive=true if false AND Processes==0 AND Threads==0 (i.e., only default HTTPKeepAlive when the entire struct appears to be zero-valued)
requirements.rationale: Operators who set spec.uwsgi: {} expect safe defaults. Operators who explicitly set httpKeepAlive=false must not have it overwritten. Since bool zero-value is false, we can only safely default HTTPKeepAlive when the whole struct is zero-valued.

scenarios.name: Empty uwsgi struct gets all defaults
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR is submitted with spec.uwsgi: {}
scenarios.then: the webhook sets processes=2, threads=2, httpKeepAlive=true

scenarios.name: Partial uwsgi gets remaining defaults
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR is submitted with spec.uwsgi.processes=4 only
scenarios.then: the webhook sets threads=2; httpKeepAlive defaults to true because the struct was not fully zero-valued (processes was explicitly set)

scenarios.name: Explicit false httpKeepAlive preserved
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR is submitted with spec.uwsgi.httpKeepAlive=false and spec.uwsgi.processes=4
scenarios.then: the webhook does NOT overwrite httpKeepAlive; processes=4, threads=2 (defaulted), httpKeepAlive=false (preserved)

scenarios.name: Nil uwsgi left nil
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR is submitted without spec.uwsgi
scenarios.then: the webhook does not create a UWSGISpec; spec.uwsgi remains nil

requirements.description: The validating webhook SHALL reject spec.uwsgi.processes < 1 and spec.uwsgi.threads < 1 as defense-in-depth alongside kubebuilder Minimum markers
requirements.rationale: Defense-in-depth validation consistent with the existing replicas, maxActiveKeys, and autoscaling validation patterns

scenarios.name: Valid uwsgi accepted
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR is submitted with spec.uwsgi.processes=4, spec.uwsgi.threads=2
scenarios.then: the validating webhook accepts the CR without errors

scenarios.name: Processes below minimum rejected
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR is submitted with spec.uwsgi.processes=0 (bypassing CRD schema)
scenarios.then: the webhook returns a field.Invalid error on spec.uwsgi.processes with message 'processes must be at least 1'

scenarios.name: Threads below minimum rejected
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR is submitted with spec.uwsgi.threads=0 (bypassing CRD schema)
scenarios.then: the webhook returns a field.Invalid error on spec.uwsgi.threads with message 'threads must be at least 1'

scenarios.name: Both invalid reports both errors
scenarios.when: spec.uwsgi.processes=0 and spec.uwsgi.threads=0
scenarios.then: the webhook returns errors for both fields

scenarios.name: Nil uwsgi skips validation
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR is submitted without spec.uwsgi
scenarios.then: no uwsgi validation errors are produced

requirements.description: The reconciler SHALL construct the uWSGI command dynamically from spec.uwsgi when non-nil, falling back to hardcoded defaults (processes=2, threads=2, keepalive=true) when nil
requirements.rationale: The reconciler must handle both new CRs with spec.uwsgi and existing CRs upgraded from before the field existed

scenarios.name: Custom uwsgi values in Deployment command
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR has spec.uwsgi.processes=4, spec.uwsgi.threads=8, spec.uwsgi.httpKeepAlive=true
scenarios.then: the Deployment container command includes --processes 4 --threads 8 --http-keepalive

scenarios.name: HTTP keep-alive disabled
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR has spec.uwsgi.httpKeepAlive=false
scenarios.then: the Deployment container command does NOT include --http-keepalive

scenarios.name: Nil uwsgi uses defaults
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR has spec.uwsgi=nil
scenarios.then: the Deployment container command includes --processes 2 --threads 2 --http-keepalive (identical to current behavior)

scenarios.name: Fixed flags are invariant
scenarios.when: any Keystone CR is reconciled regardless of spec.uwsgi
scenarios.then: the command always includes --http :5000, --wsgi-file, --master, --lazy-apps, --need-app, --pyargv

requirements.description: The generated CRD manifest SHALL be regenerated via make generate && make manifests after type changes
requirements.rationale: DeepCopy methods and CRD YAML must be kept in sync with Go types

scenarios.name: DeepCopy generated
scenarios.when: make generate is run after adding UWSGISpec
scenarios.then: zz_generated.deepcopy.go contains DeepCopyInto for UWSGISpec

scenarios.name: CRD manifest updated
scenarios.when: make manifests is run after adding UWSGISpec
scenarios.then: config/crd/bases/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml contains the uwsgi field schema

requirements.description: Integration tests SHALL verify that a Keystone CR with spec.uwsgi values passes webhook validation and that the defaulting webhook populates sub-fields correctly via envtest
requirements.rationale: envtest integration tests validate the full admission pipeline (CRD schema + defaulting webhook + validating webhook) in a real API server

scenarios.name: CR with custom uwsgi accepted
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR with spec.uwsgi.processes=4, spec.uwsgi.threads=4 is created via envtest client
scenarios.then: the CR is accepted and can be retrieved with the specified values

scenarios.name: CR with empty uwsgi gets defaults
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR with spec.uwsgi: {} is created via envtest client
scenarios.then: the retrieved CR has spec.uwsgi.processes=2, spec.uwsgi.threads=2, spec.uwsgi.httpKeepAlive=true

scenarios.name: CR without uwsgi accepted
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR without spec.uwsgi is created via envtest client
scenarios.then: the CR is accepted and spec.uwsgi remains nil

requirements.description: E2E tests SHALL verify that Deployment container args reflect CRD uwsgi values end-to-end
requirements.rationale: E2E tests validate the complete flow from CR submission through webhook defaulting to reconciler Deployment construction

scenarios.name: Default uwsgi args in Deployment
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR without spec.uwsgi reaches Ready state
scenarios.then: the Deployment container command includes --processes 2 --threads 2 --http-keepalive

scenarios.name: Custom uwsgi args after patch
scenarios.when: the Keystone CR is patched with spec.uwsgi.processes=4, spec.uwsgi.threads=8
scenarios.then: the Deployment container command is updated to include --processes 4 --threads 8

requirements.description: The Keystone CRD API reference documentation SHALL be updated to include the spec.uwsgi field, its sub-fields, defaults, and validation rules
requirements.rationale: Operators need to know the available fields, their defaults, and constraints to configure uWSGI properly

scenarios.name: Documentation includes uwsgi field
scenarios.when: an operator reads docs/reference/keystone-crd.md
scenarios.then: the documentation describes spec.uwsgi with processes, threads, httpKeepAlive fields, their types, defaults, and validation constraints

tasks.title: Add UWSGISpec struct and *UWSGISpec field to KeystoneSpec in keystone_types.go (REQ-001)
tasks.description: Define UWSGISpec struct with Processes (int32, +kubebuilder:validation:Minimum=1, +kubebuilder:default=2), Threads (int32, +kubebuilder:validation:Minimum=1, +kubebuilder:default=2), HTTPKeepAlive (bool, +kubebuilder:default=true). Add optional *UWSGISpec pointer field on KeystoneSpec between Resources and ExtraConfig, following the existing FederationSpec/AutoscalingSpec pattern. Run make generate && make manifests to regenerate deepcopy and CRD YAML.

tasks.title: Add webhook defaulting for spec.uwsgi sub-fields in keystone_webhook.go (REQ-002)
tasks.description: In KeystoneWebhook.Default(), add logic after the Resources defaulting block: when obj.Spec.UWSGI is non-nil, default Processes to 2 if 0, Threads to 2 if 0. For HTTPKeepAlive (bool, zero=false), only default to true when the entire struct is zero-valued (Processes==0 && Threads==0 && !HTTPKeepAlive). Export DefaultUWSGIProcesses=int32(2), DefaultUWSGIThreads=int32(2), DefaultUWSGIHTTPKeepAlive=true as package-level constants. Add unit tests in keystone_webhook_test.go for: (1) nil uwsgi stays nil, (2) empty uwsgi gets all defaults, (3) partial uwsgi gets remaining defaults, (4) explicit values preserved, (5) explicit httpKeepAlive=false with processes>0 preserved.

tasks.title: Add webhook validation for spec.uwsgi in keystone_webhook.go (REQ-003)
tasks.description: In KeystoneWebhook.validate(), add defense-in-depth validation block after the Resources validation: when k.Spec.UWSGI is non-nil, reject Processes < 1 and Threads < 1 with field.Invalid errors on specPath.Child("uwsgi", "processes") and specPath.Child("uwsgi", "threads"). Add unit tests in keystone_webhook_test.go for: (1) valid uwsgi accepted, (2) processes=0 rejected, (3) threads=0 rejected, (4) both invalid reports both errors, (5) nil uwsgi accepted.

tasks.title: Modify buildKeystoneDeployment to construct uWSGI command dynamically from spec.uwsgi (REQ-004)
tasks.description: In reconcile_deployment.go, replace the hardcoded Command slice in buildKeystoneDeployment with a helper function uwsgiCommand(uwsgi *UWSGISpec) []string that builds the command dynamically. When uwsgi is nil, use defaults (processes=2, threads=2, keepalive=true). When non-nil, use the provided values. The --http-keepalive flag is included only when HTTPKeepAlive is true. Fixed flags (--http :5000, --wsgi-file, --master, --lazy-apps, --need-app, --pyargv) remain invariant. Add unit tests in reconcile_deployment_test.go: (1) nil uwsgi produces default command, (2) custom processes/threads, (3) httpKeepAlive=false omits flag, (4) httpKeepAlive=true includes flag, (5) verify fixed flags always present.

tasks.title: Add envtest integration tests for uwsgi field admission pipeline (REQ-006)
tasks.description: In operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/integration_test.go, add integration tests: (1) TestIntegration_UWSGICustomValuesAccepted — create CR with spec.uwsgi.processes=4, spec.uwsgi.threads=4, verify accepted and values preserved, (2) TestIntegration_UWSGIEmptyDefaulted — create CR with spec.uwsgi: {}, verify retrieved CR has processes=2, threads=2, httpKeepAlive=true, (3) TestIntegration_UWSGINilPreserved — create CR without spec.uwsgi, verify spec.uwsgi remains nil after retrieval. Follow existing integration test patterns (setupEnvTest, validIntegrationKeystone, SkipIfEnvTestUnavailable).

tasks.title: Add Chainsaw E2E test for uwsgi field propagation to Deployment (REQ-007)
tasks.description: Create tests/e2e/keystone/uwsgi/ directory with: (1) chainsaw-test.yaml — 4-step test: apply baseline CR without uwsgi, assert Deployment command contains --processes 2 --threads 2 --http-keepalive; patch CR with spec.uwsgi.processes=4 spec.uwsgi.threads=8, assert Deployment command updated. (2) 00-keystone-cr.yaml — baseline CR fixture without spec.uwsgi. (3) 01-patch-custom-uwsgi.yaml — patch with custom uwsgi values. Follow the existing tests/e2e/keystone/resources/ pattern exactly.

tasks.title: Update docs/reference/keystone-crd.md with spec.uwsgi documentation (REQ-008)
tasks.description: Add a spec.uwsgi section to docs/reference/keystone-crd.md documenting: UWSGISpec fields (processes, threads, httpKeepAlive), types, defaults, validation constraints (Minimum=1), defaulting behavior (nil vs empty vs partial), and example YAML snippets. Follow the existing documentation pattern for spec.resources and spec.autoscaling sections. Add CC-0040 to the feature list in the frontmatter.

review_criteria: UWSGISpec struct follows the established optional pointer field pattern (*FederationSpec, *AutoscalingSpec, *corev1.ResourceRequirements) with correct kubebuilder markers (+optional, +kubebuilder:validation:Minimum=1, +kubebuilder:default=N)

Webhook defaulting correctly handles all zero-value edge cases: nil uwsgi (untouched), empty struct (all defaults), partial struct (remaining defaults), explicit false httpKeepAlive (preserved when processes or threads are non-zero)
Defense-in-depth validation follows the existing pattern (alongside kubebuilder markers) with field.Invalid errors using specPath.Child("uwsgi", "fieldName")
Reconciler produces IDENTICAL Deployment command to current behavior when spec.uwsgi is nil — no behavioral change for existing CRs
All tests pass: unit tests (webhook defaulting, webhook validation, deployment command), integration tests (envtest admission pipeline), E2E tests (Chainsaw deployment args)
Generated artifacts are up to date: make generate && make manifests succeed, zz_generated.deepcopy.go and CRD YAML include UWSGISpec
golangci-lint and go vet pass with zero errors
docs/reference/keystone-crd.md updated with spec.uwsgi field documentation
implementation_notes:
## Architecture

This feature adds a single optional field to the Keystone CRD following the established pattern for optional struct pointer fields. The change touches 3 layers:

```
Keystone CR (spec.uwsgi)
  │
  ├── Admission Pipeline
  │   ├── CRD Schema (kubebuilder markers: Minimum=1, default=2/true)
  │   ├── Defaulting Webhook (populate zero-valued sub-fields)
  │   └── Validating Webhook (defense-in-depth: processes >= 1, threads >= 1)
  │
  └── Reconciler (reconcile_deployment.go)
      └── uwsgiCommand() helper → Container.Command slice
          └── Deployment spec
```

## Key Design Decisions

1. **Optional pointer field**: spec.uwsgi is `*UWSGISpec` (not embedded), so nil means 'use defaults'. This preserves backward compatibility — existing CRs without the field continue to work identically.

2. **HTTPKeepAlive defaulting**: Since bool zero-value is `false`, we cannot distinguish 'not set' from 'explicitly false'. The defaulting webhook only sets HTTPKeepAlive=true when the ENTIRE struct is zero-valued (Processes==0 && Threads==0 && !HTTPKeepAlive). When any other field is non-zero, httpKeepAlive is left as-is. This means operators who want to disable keep-alive MUST also explicitly set processes or threads.

3. **uwsgiCommand helper**: Extract the command construction into a standalone helper function that takes `*UWSGISpec` and returns `[]string`. This makes the logic unit-testable independently of the full buildKeystoneDeployment flow.

4. **No webhook-level defaulting when nil**: Unlike Resources (which defaults to full ResourceRequirements when nil), uwsgi remains nil when unset. The reconciler handles nil by using hardcoded defaults. This is intentional — we want existing CRs to continue working without any field appearing in their spec.

## Files to Modify

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go` | Add UWSGISpec struct + UWSGI field on KeystoneSpec |
| `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_webhook.go` | Add defaulting + validation logic |
| `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_webhook_test.go` | Add 10 webhook tests |
| `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/integration_test.go` | Add 3 envtest integration tests |
| `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go` | Add uwsgiCommand helper, use in buildKeystoneDeployment |
| `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment_test.go` | Add 5 command construction tests |
| `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/zz_generated.deepcopy.go` | Auto-generated by make generate |
| `operators/keystone/config/crd/bases/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml` | Auto-generated by make manifests |
| `tests/e2e/keystone/uwsgi/` | New E2E test directory with 3 files |
| `docs/reference/keystone-crd.md` | Add spec.uwsgi documentation section |

## Patterns to Follow

- **Type definition**: See AutoscalingSpec (keystone_types.go:112-136) for kubebuilder markers on optional sub-fields
- **Webhook defaulting**: See Resources defaulting (keystone_webhook.go:78-93) for the nil-check-then-set pattern
- **Webhook validation**: See autoscaling validation (keystone_webhook.go:235-289) for defense-in-depth with field.Invalid
- **Reconciler helper**: See containerResources() (reconcile_deployment.go:286-295) for nil-safe pointer dereference
- **Unit tests**: See TestReconcileDeployment_ContainerResources (reconcile_deployment_test.go:778-790) for deployment assertion pattern
- **Integration tests**: See TestIntegration_ValidCRAccepted (integration_test.go:73-90) for envtest pattern
- **E2E tests**: See tests/e2e/keystone/resources/ for Chainsaw test structure

## Potential Pitfalls

1. **Bool zero-value ambiguity**: Go's bool zero-value is false, making it impossible to distinguish 'not set' from 'explicitly false'. The defaulting logic must account for this by only defaulting httpKeepAlive when the entire struct is zero-valued.

2. **Existing test breakage**: The existing TestReconcileDeployment_DeploymentSpec test does not assert the Command field, so it should not break. However, the readyDeployment() helper calls buildKeystoneDeployment() directly, so any signature changes must be reflected there.

3. **CRD schema defaults**: The +kubebuilder:default markers on UWSGISpec sub-fields mean the CRD schema will inject defaults for sub-fields when spec.uwsgi is provided (e.g., `uwsgi: {processes: 4}` gets threads=2 from schema default). This works in concert with webhook defaulting for defense-in-depth.
